title: Kiki Smith Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on this object.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which common theme is addressed in the work of Kiki Smith?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Smith's  *Lying with the Wolf*  brings together several themes the artist  has addressed throughout her career.  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c52fd202a704b7879bdf9661c1b52aa8.jpg)

All of these themes are common in Kiki Smith's work.  

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What technique did Kiki Smith use in *Lying with the Wolf*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Domesticity, fragility, and the humble materials of craft and folk arts feature strongly in Smith's work.

*Lying with the Wolf* is a delicate large scale drawing made with pencil and ink on paper.  

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which biographical detail has been most influential in Kiki Smith's art?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Smith speaks fondly of the Victorian house in which she was raised, and how it captured her young imagination. The notion of “home” has been central to her practice, and she likens it to the human body, a theme that is pervasive across her oeuvre.

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**

choices.content: Her childhood in a Victorian house
Her interest in opera and music

assessment_items.question_content:
The woman in *Lying with the Wolf*  is a likely reference to

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c52fd202a704b7879bdf9661c1b52aa8.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Ste. Geneviève, the patron saint of Paris, was associated with St. Francis of Assisi for her close relationships with animals and her ability, in particular, to domesticate wolves.

The figure in Smith's drawing combines references to the story of Little Red Riding Hood and the figure of Ste. her close relationships with animals and her ability, in particular, to domesticate wolves.

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does Kiki Smith's *Lying with the Wolf* suggest a feminist approach to narrative?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c52fd202a704b7879bdf9661c1b52aa8.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The nude female body was historically objectified by male artists and viewers.
Cultural traditions of the past posit women to be more closely associated to nature than men.

Smith demonstrates the slippery relationship between a visual image and its multiple references, adopting a narrative style indebted to feminist re-writings of history.  By fragmenting and combining traditional narratives in *Lying with the Wolf*, she acknowledges the possibility of multiple interpretations.  

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**

choices.content: It highlights the spirituality inherent in nature.
It focuses on the nude female body.
It is an aggressive depiction of women.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which theme in Kiki Smith's work is NOT evident in ALL of these examples?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/50392658fe9183794591bf3b3f7bee76.jpg)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7849c2cc522a0a85b8ad6a09e321da5f.jpg)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95c5f7a2b8c9bcc8aeb7edb6b5957787.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Feminist theories of the “abject" conceive of the body as a messy, porous, and boundary-less system.

All three works highlight Smith's interest in feminism, sexual identity and nature.  *Blood Pool*, however, features a small, apparently violated figure, huddled into a fetal position on the floor in reference to feminist theories of abjection.  

**[Read this text to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1960---age-of-post-colonialism/Performance-art-tutorial/a/kiki-smith-lying-with-the-wolf-2001)**
